Fern Polrrrcnr. PRACTTcBs C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
Iuly 10,2002
Teresa Vig Rein
Rein & Rein
7$ ruth Street
Modesto, CA95354
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A=02-124
Dear Ms. Rein:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Stanislaus County
regarding the disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").I
- 
auEsTroN
Are members of the Business and Workforce Alliance of Stanislaus County
public officials who must file Statements ofEconomic Interests?
CONCLUSION
Members of the Business and Workforce Alliance of Stanislaus County, which
will serve as a workforce investment board pursuant to the Workforce Investment Act of
1998, are public officials who must file Statements of Economic Interests and are subject
to the Act's conflict-of-interest rules.
FACTS
You are under alegal services contract with the County of Stanislaus to ensure
compliance with all laws relating to the Business and Workforce Alliance of Stanislaus
County, Inc. ("A11iance"), a nonprofit 501(c)(6) corporation. You have been asked by the
Stanislaus County Counsel'to obtain advice on whether proposed board members of the
1 Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Tifle 2, sections 18109-
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 The County Counsel has responsibility for providing legal services in civil matters to the Board of
Supervisors. The Board of Supervisors is the code rwiewing body for the Alliance. (Section 82011; See
Discussion.)
--


File No. A-02-124
Page No. 2
Alliance will be required to file Statements of Economic Interests. The Alliance has not
been formed. If formed, the Alliance will have a board of between 4I and 47 members
which will perform two key functions: 1) compliance with the Workforce Investment Act
of 1998 (\MIA), with funding from the federal WIA program through the WIA fiscal
agenl, Stanislaus County; and 2) economic development activities, including business
attraction, retention and marketing, with funding from local government and private
businesses.
You have stated that one of the main functions ofthe Alliance will be to serve as
a workforce investment board ("WIB") pursuant to the WIA. For example, the Alliance
will designate or procure "one stop operators" for the workforce area and will negotiate
and execute "Memoranda of Understanding" with the required one-stop partners,
collectively or severally, to provide services in the workforce delivery system.
Additionally, the Alliance will "chartef'the designated One-Stop Workforce
Development System sites. Performance standards will be established, including a
customer satisfaction index, which will be used to evaluate the effectiveness of the site in
achieving the goals and outcomes established in the WIA's required Five Year Plan.
ANALYSIS
Section 87300 provides that
"Every agency shall adopt and promulgate a Conflict of
Interest Code pursuant to the provisions of this article. A
Conflict of Interest Code shall have the force of law and
any violation of a Conflict of Interest Code by a designated
employee shall be deemed a violation of this chapter."
"Agency" means any state agency or local government agency. (Section 82003.)
We have previously advised lhat aWIB formed pursuant to the Workforce Investment
Act is a local government ageflcy under the Act. (Holland Advice Letter, No. I-01-140;
lulartin Advice Letter, No. 4-99-289; see also Dostarr Advice Letter, No. A-00-022.)
Because the Alliance serves as a WIB, it is a local government agency for purposes of the
Act. As public officials,3 all of the members of the Alliance's board are subject to the
Act's conflict-of-interest rules since they will make or participate in the making of
governmental decisions. (Section 87100; Dostart, supra; Baird Advice Letter, No. A-94-
299.) Additionally, since the members will make or participate in the making of
governmental decisions, they will be "designated employees"a subject to the Act's
disclosure rules and required to submit Statements of Economic Interests.
' "Public official" is defined as every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local
govemment agency, wilh certain exceptions not applicable here. (Section 82048.)
4 A "designated employee" includes a member of an agency whose position with the agency is designated
in a Conflict of Interest Code because the position entails the making or participation in the making of
decisions which may foreseeably have a material effect on any financial interest. (Section S2019(c).)


File No. A-02-124
Page No. 3
Please be aware that whether a separate code is established or the officials are
included in Stanislaus County's code is a determination to be made by the Board of
Supervisors, the code reviewing body. (Sections 87301 and 8201 l.) If you have any
other questions regarding this matter or need technical assistance regarding the
establishment or amendment of a code, please contact the Division of Technical
Assistance at (9 I 6) 322-5 660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
Natalie B
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

sections.conclusion:
Members of the Business and Workforce Alliance of Stanislaus County, which
will serve as a workforce investment board pursuant to the Workforce Investment Act of
1998, are public officials who must file Statements of Economic Interests and are subject
to the Act's conflict-of-interest rules.

employee shall be deemed a violation of this chapter."

CONCLUSION: Members of the Business and Workforce Alliance of Stanislaus County, which
will serve as a workforce investment board pursuant to the Workforce Investment Act of
1998, are public officials who must file Statements of Economic Interests and are subject
to the Act's conflict-of-interest rules.
